summary: The team is in full execution mode, prioritizing the Asia Start and Intelligent China programs while launching the joint IE report on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, with urgent SEO setup and campaign finalization this week.
summaryDetail: The biggest push right now is the dual-track Asia Start campaign (AI route led by Joseph, traditional by Daisy) and the Intelligent China program launch, with the webpage going live this week and SEO efforts starting immediately. The joint IE report, titled 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global', is on a tight timeline for a September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore—Felix is finalizing the one-pager and meeting Tech in Asia this week. Urgent this week: finalize Asia Start campaign routes, get Intelligent China website ready, and fix ad campaigns from audience targeting to observation mode. Upcoming: the Shun Tian Shu press release incorporating 10 points publishes July 6, the social media agency selection moves to the next round with all four agencies invited, and planning begins for Professor Zhu Rui's new book and Dean Xiang's keynote at QS Summit in Bali.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Strategy
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for key topics like AI and EVs in China, and prioritizes Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson events in Singapore in September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and a new 'Intelligent China' program launching in December. Need to finalize campaign strategy and website.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in shaping cooperation.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; SEO efforts to begin immediately.
Shun Tian Shu press release will incorporate the 10 points rather than creating a separate article.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints and time.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Target to have digital version ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and focuses on module one (Beijing) initially.
Invite all four agencies (Mai Zhi, Sheng Guang, Impactable, Media Minds Global) to the next round of the social media ad agency selection.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: Request for Proposal - social media ad agency 2026 - updated 0703

decisions.text: Use Temu as the second case study for the IE University report instead of halocoko.
Place key findings at the beginning of the IE University report.
Proceed with the shorter version of the article (around 1500 words) for Erica's approval.
decisions.meetingTitle: FW: 长江商学院媒体监测日报2026-7-2
